changes.reason: Batch D only. The latest accepted charm-wall geometry improved score, net profit, drawdown, worst MAE, and PnL/exposure; raising higher-low confirmation from 0.5 to 1 keeps the admission expansion tied to actual ES support rather than loose wall chasing.
Expand the bounded charm-wall acceptance box from 20 to 25 to test whether nearby supported breakaways add sample count and convex PnL without touching global vetoes or option-data fallback behavior.

Offset the wider charm-wall box with a local VWAP-extension cap at 22. The profile is efficient but tail-heavy on average MAE, so this avoids admitting the farthest chase entries while still allowing the current median extension zone.

expected_effect: Should add or preserve high-quality charm_wall_breakaway_long trades while improving profile admission quality and keeping the convex 12-point target intact. Main tradeoff is that the stronger higher-low and tighter VWAP cap may remove one existing profitable wall-breakaway trade; reject if total net profit falls below 98% of run_029, profile PnL/exposure drops below 225, or total worst MAE worsens beyond -513.4.
label: promote
rationale: Use exactly one legal family: batch_d_charm_wall_breakaway_long. The portfolio leader is run_029, while run_036 shows that charm-wall admission geometry is one of the few recent mechanisms that improved objective without starving trades. This is a non-duplicate follow-through: widen the wall-distance box, require stronger ES higher-low support, and cap extreme VWAP extension locally. It avoids failed direct 1DTE tightening, failed squeeze compression, stale fallback compression, broad pullback expansion, and global veto tightening.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long produced 5 VALID_0DTE trades, $985.6 PnL, $197.1 average PnL, 234.36 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, no slow >=12h trades, and worst MAE -346.8. The previous geometry move improved score by 110.6 and net profit by $72.5 without reducing trade count.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Wall Supported Admission Follow-Through
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Small sample risk is high; the wider charm-wall box can admit lower-quality continuation attempts, while the VWAP cap or higher-low requirement can remove existing convex winners.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm-wall breakaway context with price above the charm wall, require-breakout still true, and ES support confirmed by higher-low structure but not excessive VWAP extension.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: charm_wall_breakaway_long PnL/exposure remains >=225, total net profit remains >=98% of run_029, completed trades decline by no more than 10%, slow_12h remains 0, and total worst MAE is no worse than -513.4.

strategy_plan_reference.market_regime_read: This is a short, high-win-rate, take-profit-only regime over about 12.3 calendar days. Realized losses and slow >=12h trades are absent, but hidden MAE remains the binding risk:...

new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Split
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= $1305, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.9, total net profit >=98% of run_029, and score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast winners, total net profit >=98% of run_029, and slow_12h remain...

new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Dormant Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile has positive standalone PnL or reduces combined charm/pin worst MAE while total net profit remains >=98% of run_029 and protected fast-winner retention...

new_strategy_ideas.name: Call Wall Acceptance Isolated Sample Builder
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 3 total call_wall_acceptance_long trades, positive standalone PnL, profile PnL/exposure >=250, worst MAE <= -150, and total net profit >=98% of run_029.
